title: Memorandum for the Record
source_text: 1. Memorandum for the Record. Use a Memorandum for the Record (MFR) as an internal document to record supporting information in the record that is not recorded elsewhere. Examples include such things as documenting the results of a meeting, an important telephone conversation, or an oral agreement. Type or handwrite these most informal memorandums. See figure 10-1. A full signature line and identification symbols are not required; however, it should be dated, signed, and show the organizational position of the signer. If it is only two or three lines, include it on the file copy of your document. Leave out the subject line if you add your MFR to the file copy.
plain_language_rule: Use a Memorandum for the Record (MFR) as an internal document to record supporting information not recorded elsewhere (e.g., meeting results, telephone conversations, oral agreements). MFRs may be typed or handwritten (most informal). Figure 10-1 shows: date at top right, heading "MEMORANDUM FOR THE RECORD", numbered body paragraphs, signer name and organizational code. A full signature line and identification symbols are not required; however, the MFR should be dated, signed, and show the signer's organizational position. Standalone MFR may include a Subj line. If the MFR is added to the file copy, omit the subject line. For two or three-line MFRs, include on the file copy.
renderer_impact: MFR may be typed or handwritten. Render date at top right for standalone MFR. Render heading "MEMORANDUM FOR THE RECORD". Support optional Subj line (omit for file-copy additions). Support numbered body paragraphs. Show signer name and organizational code/position. Does not require full signature line or identification symbols.
notes: Source text resolved from SECNAV M-5216.5 CH-1, 10-2.1 Memorandum for the Record. Key points: (1) MFR is for recording supporting information not recorded elsewhere, (2) examples: meeting results, telephone conversations, oral agreements, (3) typed or handwritten, (4) no full signature line or identification symbols required, (5) should be dated, signed, show organizational position, (6) for two or three lines, include on file copy, (7) omit subject line for file copy additions. Figure 10-1 details: date at top right, heading "MEMORANDUM FOR THE RECORD", numbered body paragraphs, signer name and organizational code. Body text says organizational position; Figure 10-1 shows organizational code — treat both as rule-bearing until reconciled. Figure details are part of source review, not optional illustration. Enforceability is partial because internal-document determination and file-copy context require external judgment.
